input.command: grep -r "parse_file\s*(" . --include="*.rs" --include="*.py" --include="*.js" --include="*.java" --include="*.cpp" --include="*.c" --include="*.go" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | grep -v ".venv/" | grep -v "Binary" | grep -v "^[^:]*:.*//.*parse_file" | cut -d: -f1 | sort -u

for file in "./src/index.rs" "./src/parser/c_lang.rs" "./src/parser/cpp.rs" "./src/parser/csharp.rs" "./src/parser/go.rs" "./src/parser/java.rs" "./src/parser/javascript.rs" "./src/parser/python.rs" "./src/parser/ruby.rs" "./src/parser/rust_lang.rs" "./src/parser/treesitter.rs" "./src/parser/typescript.rs"; do
  # Check if the file has parse_file calls (not definitions)
  if grep -q "parse_file\s*(" "$file" && ! grep -q "^pub fn parse_file\|^fn parse_file" "$file"; then
    echo "$file"
  elif grep "parse_file\s*(" "$file" | grep -v "^pub fn parse_file\|^fn parse_file" | grep -q "."; then
    echo "$file"
  fi
done
